mnemonics.title: Fetch-Decode-Execute Cycle Steps
mnemonics.content: REMEMBER THE THREE STEPS AS: FIRST FIND THE INSTRUCTION (FETCH), THEN UNDERSTAND WHAT TO DO (DECODE), THEN DO IT (EXECUTE). THINK OF IT LIKE READING A RECIPE: GET THE RECIPE, UNDERSTAND IT, COOK THE FOOD.

mnemonics.title: CPU Components
mnemonics.content: REMEMBER CPU HAS THREE MAIN PARTS: CAL (ALU FOR CALCULATIONS), CU (CONTROL UNIT FOR CONTROLLING), REGISTERS (FOR QUICK STORAGE). THINK: 'C-U-R' = CALCULATE, UNDERSTAND, REMEMBER.

mnemonics.title: Bus Types
mnemonics.content: THREE BUSES: A FOR ADDRESS (WHERE TO GO), D FOR DATA (WHAT TO CARRY), C FOR CONTROL (HOW TO OPERATE). REMEMBER: ADC = ADDRESS CARRIES DESTINATION, DATA CARRIES CONTENT, CONTROL CARRIES COMMANDS.

acronyms.usage: The main processor that executes instructions in a computer system
The component that performs arithmetic and logical operations within the CPU
Directs operations of the processor by generating control signals
Holds the address of the next instruction to be fetched
Holds the address of the memory location to be accessed
Temporarily holds data being transferred to/from memory
A register used to store intermediate results of arithmetic operations
Volatile primary memory that can be read from and written to
The collection of all machine instructions a CPU can execute

visualTips.visualization: DRAW THE CPU AS A TRIANGLE: AT THE TOP IS THE CONTROL UNIT (THE BRAIN'S LEADER), AT THE BOTTOM LEFT IS THE ALU (THE WORKER), AND AT THE BOTTOM RIGHT ARE REGISTERS (THE TOOLBOX). ARROWS SHOW DATA FLOW BETWEEN THEM.
IMAGINE A FACTORY ASSEMBLY LINE: FETCH = GETTING RAW MATERIALS FROM WAREHOUSE, DECODE = READING THE INSTRUCTIONS ON HOW TO PROCESS, EXECUTE = ACTUALLY BUILDING THE PRODUCT.

DRAW AN INVERTED PYRAMID: AT THE TOP (SMALLEST, FASTEST) ARE CPU REGISTERS, BELOW IS CACHE, BELOW IS RAM (MAIN MEMORY), AT THE BOTTOM (LARGEST, SLOWEST) IS HARD DISK. THE WIDTH REPRESENTS CAPACITY, HEIGHT REPRESENTS SPEED.
THINK OF A CITY BUS SYSTEM: ADDRESS BUS IS LIKE STREET NAMES (WHERE TO GO), DATA BUS IS LIKE THE BUS ITSELF CARRYING PASSENGERS (DATA), CONTROL BUS IS LIKE TRAFFIC LIGHTS AND SIGNS (WHEN TO GO).

analogies.analogy: THE CPU IS LIKE A KITCHEN CHEF AND MEMORY IS LIKE THE PANTRY. THE CHEF (CPU) READS RECIPES (INSTRUCTIONS) FROM THE PANTRY (MEMORY), PREPARES FOOD, AND RETURNS IT TO THE PANTRY OR SERVES IT.

REGISTERS ARE LIKE THE COUNTERTOP WHERE YOU ARE IMMEDIATELY WORKING, WHILE MAIN MEMORY IS LIKE THE REFRIGERATOR ACROSS THE KITCHEN. YOU CAN ACCESS COUNTERTOP ITEMS MUCH FASTER, BUT YOU HAVE LESS SPACE THERE.
THE INSTRUCTION SET IS LIKE THE VOCABULARY OF A LANGUAGE. ARCHITECTURE DEFINES THE WORDS (INSTRUCTIONS) AVAILABLE, WHILE ORGANIZATION IS LIKE THE GRAMMAR AND SYNTAX OF HOW THE WORDS ARE USED.
THE CONTROL UNIT IS LIKE THE CONDUCTOR OF AN ORCHESTRA. IT DOES NOT PLAY ANY INSTRUMENT ITSELF BUT DIRECTS WHEN EACH SECTION (ALU, REGISTERS, MEMORY) SHOULD PLAY THEIR PART.
